steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Boundaries question. The blank must complete the first independent clause (“The vessel took six days to ______”) and then punctuate correctly before the second independent clause (“it’s as heavy as two thousand blue whales when fully loaded”). The second clause explains why dislodging took so long (the ship’s enormous weight), so the punctuation must join two independent clauses where the second elaborates on the first.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — dislodge in part due to its sheer size,: a comma alone joins two independent clauses, creating a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — dislodge, in part due to its sheer size:: the comma correctly sets off the parenthetical phrase “in part due to its sheer size,” and the colon correctly introduces the elaborating independent clause that explains the delay. ✓
  • Choice C — dislodge, in part due to its sheer size,: a comma alone at the end joins two independent clauses, creating a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — dislodge, in part, due to its sheer size: no terminal punctuation separates “its sheer size” from “it’s as heavy as…”, running two independent clauses together without any boundary. ✗
That is the correct answer.
